html:
KRONO is no longer a net-cash layup, and the old RM30 million earnings-power story is too aggressive. The better thesis is a post-write-down turnaround with real option value: at RM0.13, the market is pricing Kronologi as a broken reseller just as the company has cleaned up the old partner-related goodwill, kept its As-A-Service revenue intact, and replaced a fading storage relationship with an exclusive Hammerspace Asia partnership aimed directly at AI data orchestration.

The January 2026 numbers look terrible on the surface: FY2026 revenue fell 31% to RM209 million and reported loss was RM238 million. But the loss was dominated by RM206 million of goodwill impairment, RM17.7 million of PPE impairment, and RM10.6 million of accelerated maintenance expense. The continuing business is weak, not dead. Before extraordinary items, FY2026 PBT was roughly breakeven, and the real split matters: EDM Infrastructure collapsed from RM232.6 million to RM139.9 million, while EDM As-A-Service was essentially flat at RM69.2 million.

That is the crux. The market is looking at the collapse in legacy infrastructure revenue. The asset that may still matter is the installed customer base, the recurring backup, recovery, monitoring, disaster recovery, and hybrid-cloud data workflow service layer across Singapore, Philippines, China, and the rest of Asia. In FY2026, As-A-Service generated segment profit while Infrastructure was loss-making. If Infrastructure merely stops bleeding and Hammerspace converts into real bookings, the earnings delta is large relative to a RM116 million market cap.

The Hammerspace partnership is not generic AI theater. Hammerspace is tied to the current bottleneck in enterprise AI: distributed data, unstructured data, GPU data access, governance, and multi-site orchestration. Kronologi’s old reseller identity is low-multiple and disposable. A regional channel and services partner for AI data platforms is not. The stock does not need the market to value KRONO like a software company. It only needs investors to stop treating the company as a terminal hardware pass-through business.

The balance sheet gives time, but not a free floor. Cash was RM34.5 million at January 2026 against RM22.6 million of borrowings and lease liabilities. Current assets of RM110.7 million exceeded total liabilities of RM96.6 million, and net assets were RM198.2 million, or RM0.27 per share on the company’s presentation. Tangible book after remaining goodwill is closer to RM140 million, still above the current market cap. The stock is not protected by idle cash alone. It is protected by a cleaned-up balance sheet, working-capital liquidity, and a valuation already discounting a lot of failure.

The forward setup is asymmetric because the base earnings bar is low. If FY2027 revenue stabilizes around RM220-240 million, gross margin holds near 22-24%, and operating costs stay around the FY2026 run rate, KRONO can return to RM8-12 million of normalized net profit. That is not spectacular, but at RM0.13 the stock would be at roughly 10-15x depressed transition earnings while trading below stated book and only modestly above tangible liquidation support. If Hammerspace pulls Infrastructure back toward RM180-200 million and As-A-Service resumes growth, RM15-20 million of earnings is plausible, and the stock becomes genuinely cheap at 6-8x forward earnings.

Retail context is also interesting. Recent local discussion is active after the bounce from the RM0.09-0.10 lows, but much of it is still trading-oriented: stop-loss chatter, questions about whether KRONO is “AI” or software, and old penny-stock momentum comments. That is not institutional sponsorship. It is exactly the kind of misunderstood, low-float, post-capitulation setup where a few clean quarters can reframe the story quickly.

The bear case is that the old partnership loss was not just accounting. If the lost technology partner contributed structurally important revenue, Hammerspace adoption is slow, and As-A-Service begins to decline, KRONO is just a shrinking reseller with too much fixed cost. In that case, FY2027 could remain near breakeven or loss-making, cash could keep leaking through leases, maintenance, and working capital, and the market will ignore book value.

The base case is more balanced: Q4 was the trough, As-A-Service holds around RM70-75 million, Infrastructure recovers partially, and the company earns RM8-12 million as the new partner stack ramps. A low-teens P/E plus a discount to RM0.27 book supports RM0.20-0.24.

The bull case is where the torque lives. If Hammerspace becomes a real APAC wedge into AI data platforms, KRONO’s legacy infrastructure segment can flip from a drag to a higher-value AI data orchestration channel. With RM260-300 million revenue, 24-26% gross margin, and modest operating leverage, RM18-25 million of net profit is possible. At 12-15x that earnings power, the stock can trade at RM0.35-0.45 without heroic assumptions.

Current price: RM0.13.

Bear target: RM0.07, down 46%.

Base target: RM0.22, up 69%.

Bull target: RM0.40, up 208%.
